finding: call_mcp_tool uuid-branch used the model-supplied server_id directly, skipping the accessible-set check the legacy path enforced — a prompt-injected tool name could execute on a server the user's groups aren't assigned to, using that server's admin secret headers (cross-group authz bypass)
a tool execution error propagated via ? and aborted the whole turn, leaving the persisted tool_use with no tool_result → next-turn history is an orphan tool_use the provider rejects

cross-request approval resume double-executed: the RegistryBridge runs MCP before_llm_call which itself executes approved tools on resume, while the loop's native resume-execution (last_pending_assistant) also executed them from stale history → tool ran twice then re-prompted forever
a partial suspend (mixed auto+prompt tools in one round) broke on the first suspend, stranding later tools with neither a result nor a pending row → orphaned tool_use + lost approval on resume
the denial check failed OPEN — a transient DB error on get_denied_tools_for_branch fell through to decide_pure, so in AutoApprove mode a denied tool could auto-execute
the single-use approval claim discarded delete_tool_approval's bool — a concurrent resume could read the same approved row and both return Auto (double-execute a mutating tool)

is_trusted name-fallback used builtin_server_id_by_name (which includes code_sandbox/control_mcp) instead of is_builtin_server_id — a name-prefixed code_sandbox tool would auto-approve code execution (latent; name path unreachable today)

the spawned generation task releases the slot + terminal frame + cancel token explicitly at the tail with no RAII/Drop guard — a panic in the task permanently latches begin_generation ('reply already generating') + leaks the cancel-token entry + hangs the SSE stream
new finish_reason wire strings for cap stops (max_steps/token_cap/timeout) are outside the legacy vocabulary (stop/cancelled) a client may switch on — cosmetic, chat caps effectively never fire

chat imports its MCP-call chokepoint (call_mcp_tool/resolve_tool_server/McpCallScope/CancelSignal) from workflow::dispatch — two sibling feature modules coupled laterally; call_mcp_tool is shared MCP infra that belongs in mcp/, not workflow | FIXED: moved the chokepoint (call_mcp_tool + resolve_tool_server + builtin name map + McpCallScope/McpToolCallError/CancelSignal/ChatCallCtx) to a new shared mcp/agent_tool_call.rs; chat (resolver/gate/dispatcher) + workflow both import from mcp/; only impl CancelSignal for RunHandle stays in workflow (owns the type). §9 DAG satisfied.

split_tool_name + mcp_to_agent_result are copy-pasted between chat resolver.rs and workflow agent_dispatch.rs and have already diverged (chat computes terminal from audience; workflow hardcodes terminal:false) | FIXED: de-duplicated into mcp/agent_tool_call.rs (single split_tool_name + mcp_to_agent_result). DIVERGENCE RECONCILED: unified on chat's audience-computed `terminal` (workflow's hardcoded terminal:false was a LATENT BUG — it ignored the audience:['user'] terminal signal on the workflow agent-step path). Real bug fix.

namespaced-tool __-splitting is re-implemented in 4 places (resolver split_tool_name, gate split_server_tool, workflow split_tool_name, call_mcp_tool uuid-parse) with no single owner for the <server_id>__<tool> convention
call_mcp_tool takes 9 positional params incl a bare enforce_conversation_disabled:bool (a security gate) + two Option<String> — boolean/Option soup, trivial to transpose or flip the gate silently
ChatAgentTurn is a 16-public-field struct with no constructor; provider/model_name/model_id/provider_id must stay mutually consistent (one create_provider_from_model_id call) but nothing enforces it
a gate-suspended turn emits Stopped(Halted) which the sink maps to finish_reason 'cancelled', conflating user-stop with awaiting-approval — StopReason has no Suspended variant
AgentExtension::on_delta is never called by the loop (streaming drives only DeltaSink); dead trait method that misleads the next porter
Budget per_step_token_cap / step_over_cap are never consulted by the loop (only unit-tested) — dead accounting carried through every Budget::new third arg

AgentTurnRequest.inputs / TurnContext.inputs (DEC-19) is threaded end-to-end but consumed by no host (both pass Null; chat carries its request via RegistryBridge.send_request) — over-engineered seam with zero current consumer
AgentEvent::ToolNotification is handled by the sink but never emitted by the loop (ToolProvider has no sink access) — dead variant + dead sink branch

workflow agent host is_trusted uses builtin_server_id_by_name (includes code_sandbox/control) -> a kind:agent step's code_sandbox call is auto-approved (Trusted->Auto), bypassing reviewer/human gate; chat twin correctly uses is_builtin_server_id which excludes them

resolve_bare_tool_server returns the FIRST accessible server advertising the bare tool name; legacy recover_server_id_for_bare_name returns None for a name advertised by >=2 servers (never guesses) -> a bare-name approval can persist the wrong server_id and resume against the wrong server
TEST-41 claims to cover the new ChatModelResolver/WorkflowModelResolver RBAC but never sets ZIEE_CHAT_AGENT_CORE=1, so it runs the LEGACY path -> the new resolver denial code is untested

multiturn test asserts history.contains('purple-turtle-42') which turn-1's tool_result already wrote -> the assertion passes even if turn-2 context recall is completely broken (does not verify its headline claim)

process-global unsafe set_var('ZIEE_CHAT_AGENT_CORE','1') with remove_var only at the end -> a panic skips teardown leaving the flag ON for subsequent tests in the same binary; --test-threads=6 default flips concurrent chat tests onto the agent-core path (cross-test contamination)
call_mcp_tool has 10 positional params incl two adjacent Option<String> (review_classification, idempotency_key) swappable with no compile error -> silent journaling corruption
chat passes Some(idem) as idempotency_key but call_mcp_tool applies it only when source==Workflow -> chat idempotency key is silently dropped (misleading no-op)

mod.rs 'integration contract' says chat tools flow via ChatToolProvider::list from tool_scope server NAMEs; actually RegistryBridge before_llm_call does request.tools=all_tools (overwrite) after the loop already called list -> ChatToolProvider::list opens a session+list_tools RPC per server per iteration and discards it; doc contract is wrong (uuid scheme, not names)

shared call_mcp_tool + chat-only ChatCallCtx live in the workflow module; chat imports crate::modules::workflow::dispatch -> DAG inversion (chat depends on workflow for its core MCP path). PRE-EXISTING coupling (call_mcp_tool was already shared here); refactor added ChatCallCtx | FIXED: chokepoint + ChatCallCtx moved to mcp/agent_tool_call.rs; workflow re-exports for its own internal callers.
mid-stream cancellation drops the partially-accumulated assistant message without transcript.append -> reload shows an empty assistant turn (streamed-vs-persisted divergence for a stopped generation)
ChatApprovalPolicy::decide runs 2 DB queries (approved+denied) per tool-call decision per step even with no pending approvals; legacy classification was in-memory

fan_out returns Err on first child error while remaining spawned children run detached (JoinHandle drop != abort); CancelToken not fired -> wasted model calls. NOTE: fanout is DESCOPED (no production caller) so not a live path

broadened is_built_in accessibility relies on 'per-tool authz enforced downstream for every built-in' but no test asserts a permission-lacking user is denied via the agent path (unverified invariant, no concrete escalation found)
dump.contains('not_found') is near-trivially true (not_found is in the citations tool schema); the real anchor is the count==0 bibliography check
ports.rs 6 traits are domain-neutral (Uuid/ai_providers/AppError only), no host/chat/DB leak; a new consumer can implement without editing the crate

is_trusted false-denies read-only built-ins (elicitation/knowledge_base/skill/tool_result) not in builtin_server_id_by_name (only 7 of 10 mapped) -> a workflow agent step using them routes through review instead of auto-bypass. PRE-EXISTING (before the Phase-7 fix too: None.is_some()=false); conservative-safe (more approvals, not a security hole); my fix only correctly changed code_sandbox
fn-level #[allow(unused_variables)] over-suppresses (only idem is unused) -> a future dead local in call() would be silenced
AgentCoreFlag #[must_use] does not fire on `let _ = AgentCoreFlag::on()` (drops immediately, flag off during test body); all 6 callers use a named binding so latent not live

PRE-EXISTING compile error: `config.postgresql.embedded.expect()` moves a non-Copy field out of a borrow -> the whole path_resolution test binary failed to build (0 tests ran). Fixed with `.as_ref()`. Real bug (deterministic, not model-flaky). red: build error -> green: 6 passed

PRE-EXISTING deterministic OFF failure: test_recall_requires_memory_enabled assumed memory is disabled-by-default, but the schema seeds enabled=true (202607140185_memory_schema.sql:17 DEFAULT true + 202607145050_memory_seed.sql:4). Recall's MEMORY_DISABLED guard (handlers.rs:415) is CORRECT; the test never actually disabled memory so recall returned {memories:[]}. Fixed: test now sets enabled=false via the DB then asserts the guard fires. red: FAILED -> green: ok

PRE-EXISTING deterministic OFF failure: helper parsed GET /conversations/{id}/messages as Vec<Value>, but the endpoint returns PaginatedMessages object {messages,has_more_before,has_more_after} (routes.rs:37->get_conversation_history -> Json<PaginatedMessages>). Stale test. Fixed to extract .messages. red: 'invalid type: map, expected a sequence' -> green: ok
PRE-EXISTING deterministic OFF failure: `body.as_array()` with stale comment 'API returns array directly' on the same PaginatedMessages endpoint. Fixed to extract .messages. red: FAILED -> green: ok

PRE-EXISTING deterministic OFF failure: BUILTIN_SUFFIXES allow-list missing __run_js (js_tool auto-attaches for tool-capable chats), so the 'only built-in tools attach' assertion failed on the run_js tool. Product behavior correct; stale allow-list. Fixed: added __run_js. red: FAILED -> green: ok

PRE-EXISTING (on main, NOT agent-core) deterministic cluster: 8 agentic_chat tests fail because attach_files_mcp is gated on manifest_available = !files.is_empty() (available_files.rs:292 -> file.rs:117), so files_mcp write tools (create_file/edit_file) never attach in an EMPTY conversation -> the model cannot author the first file (chicken-and-egg); combined memory+files tests hit the same. Also the StubChat harness has no create_file plan arm. Root-caused but NOT fixed here: the fix (decouple files_mcp tool-attach from file-presence) is a core-chat product-behavior change with wide blast radius (changes the attached-tool set for EVERY conversation, breaks other committed assertions e.g. the elicitation empty-config test) and a genuine product decision, outside the agent-core diff. SURFACED for a scoping decision (see PREEXISTING_BUGS.md).

NOT a code bug: chat_path_tool_call_records_source_chat fails at the `tool_starts > 0` GUARD (the test itself documents this distinguishes 'an LLM/setup miss' from a recording bug). The weak local Qwen bridge did not emit the tool call. MODEL-FLAKY, classified not-a-bug.
